name: CBRE Commercial Real Estate Data API
what_it_does: CBRE data and analytics APIs for commercial real estate market intelligence platform. Enables AI agents to manage CRE market vacancy and availability data retrieval for market analysis, handle transaction and comparable sale data for investment analytics, access construction pipeline and new supply data for market forecasting, retrieve occupier and tenant demand tracking for leasing strategy, manage portfolio analytics and asset performance data for CRE investors, handle CBRE Research market reports and analytics data, access office, industrial, retail, and multifamily sector metrics, retrieve capital markets activity and investment volumes for CRE finance, manage corporate occupier benchmark data for workplace strategy, and integrate CRE market intelligence with investment management and portfolio analytics platforms.
use_cases: Analyzing CRE market statistics from portfolio strategy agents
Retrieving investment transactions from acquisition analytics agents
Tracking occupier demand from leasing strategy agents
Benchmarking assets from portfolio management agents
not_for: Residential real estate without commercial CRE institutional focus
Consumer property search without institutional CRE analytics depth
Markets outside CBRE coverage (limited in some emerging markets)
best_when: An institutional CRE investor, REIT, or corporate occupier using CBRE services wants AI agents to automate CRE market analysis, investment transaction research, occupier analytics, and portfolio benchmarking.
avoid_when: LICENSING RISK: CBRE research data is proprietary and redistribution requires authorization. CRE market data methodology differs by provider; cross-provider comparison requires methodology alignment. Property valuations for lending and financial reporting require MAI-certified appraisal regardless of market data analytics.

auth.notes: CBRE data API uses OAuth 2.0 and API key. Enterprise client relationship required. CBREHost is their occupier analytics platform with API. Limited public API documentation. No webhooks. Dallas, Texas HQ. Public (CBRE NYSE). World's largest CRE services firm by revenue.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — client relationship required for data access
pricing.paid_tiers: Data licensing as part of advisory services engagement or standalone data agreement
pricing.notes: Dallas, Texas. Founded 1906. Public (CBRE NYSE). $30B+ annual revenue. World's largest CRE services firm. 130,000+ employees in 100+ countries. CBRE Research produces leading market reports. CBRE Host for occupier analytics. Competes with JLL and Cushman & Wakefield for global CRE services.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; client-negotiated

known_agent_gotchas: LICENSING RISK: CBRE research data redistribution without authorization violates data agreements; validate use rights before building downstream data products
Relationship-gated — CBRE data API requires active client relationship; standalone data API access without advisory engagement requires separate negotiation
No formal developer program — CBRE data API is not a formal public developer platform; limited public documentation; expect direct CBRE relationship for API access
Data vintage — CRE market statistics are quarterly; real-time market data unavailable; validate data recency for time-sensitive analytics
CBRE Host vs. Research — CBRE Host is occupier analytics; CBRE Research is market data; these are separate platforms with different API access paths
Appraisal requirement — automated valuation from market data is not a substitute for MAI-certified appraisal for lending, GAAP fair value, or insurance purposes
agent_readiness.security_notes: CRE market intelligence. SOC2, GDPR. OAuth2. US/EU/APAC. Commercial property and market data.

security_score_components.security_notes: CRE market data. SOC2, GDPR. OAuth2. US/EU. Commercial real estate data.
